items.mistake: Replacing GELU with ReLU when loading a BERT or GPT-2 checkpoint
items.fix: Keep `nn.GELU()` (or the model config's `hidden_act`) in FFN layers; only change activations in newly added heads if you retrain those parts.
items.why: Pretrained weights assume GELU statistics and gradient paths; ReLU changes the post-activation distribution abruptly.
items.example: Fine-tuning `bert-base-uncased` with ReLU in `BertIntermediate` typically hurts convergence compared to leaving `hidden_act='gelu'`.

items.mistake: Assuming GELU and Swish (SiLU) are the same function
items.fix: Use the correct formula: GELU(x) = x·Φ(x); Swish is x·σ(x). They are similar in shape but not identical.
items.why: Swapping them in a pretrained model changes every FFN output and breaks weight compatibility.
items.example: At x=1.0, GELU ≈ 0.84 and Swish ≈ 0.73—close but not interchangeable when weights were trained on GELU.

items.mistake: Treating tanh and sigmoid GELU approximations as exactly equal
items.fix: Stick to one approximation (or exact erf) for training, eval, and ONNX export; document which variant your checkpoint uses.
items.why: BERT-style tanh and sigmoid shortcuts differ by up to ~1e-3 on some inputs—usually fine within one stack, problematic when mixing frameworks.
items.example: Exporting a PyTorch model with `approximate='tanh'` to a runtime that implements the sigmoid form can shift logits slightly at the margin.
